research.product: SAP Cloud Integration (SAP Integration Suite) — OData API, Message Processing Logs
research.interface: OData API v2 at <tenant>/api/v1, MessageProcessingLogs API 1.0.0 (OData 2.0)

official_sources.title: Query Options | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror of help.sap.com)
official_sources.used_for: supported system query options ($filter/$select/$orderby/$top/$skip/$inlinecount/$expand); MPL $filter examples (Status eq 'ERROR', LogEnd gt datetime'...' and LogStart lt datetime'...'); 'parsing of time zone information in datetime literals is not supported. All datetime literals are interpreted as UTC'; server-side paging: max 1000 entries per call, responses over the cap carry a next link with $skiptoken; browser %20 space-encoding note

official_sources.title: HTTP Calls and URI Components | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: service root URI = service-key url parameter + /api/v1; call = service root + resource path + query options; example /api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs?$filter=Status eq 'COMPLETED'

official_sources.title: Message Processing Logs | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: MPL resources (Logs, Error Information, Custom Header Properties, Adapter Attributes); MonitoringDataRead role template for API clients; DISCARDED logs carry no error information; X-CSRF-Token: Fetch flow for modifying calls

official_sources.title: Message Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: the complete documented Status enum: COMPLETED, PROCESSING, RETRY, ESCALATED, FAILED, CANCELLED, DISCARDED, ABANDONED

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Message Processing Logs (Version 1.0.0)
official_sources.used_for: API name/version (MessageProcessingLogs 1.0.0), 'implemented based on OData version 2 specification', service URL shape .../api/v1

official_sources.title: JSON Format (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: d wrapper; results array for collections; __next partial-set link semantics; __deferred navigation representation; /Date(ms)/ Edm.DateTime encoding

official_sources.title: Runtime Artifacts and Error Information Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: the .../ErrorInformation/$value pattern returning the error text for an entity

research.language_rationale: The ops tooling this monitor extends is TypeScript on Node (sap/client.ts ships here); SAP documents the Cloud Integration API as plain OData 2.0 over HTTP with basic/OAuth auth and d-envelope JSON, and SAP publishes no supported Node SDK for this API, so Node's built-in fetch against the documented wire contract is the credible dependency-free surface.

verified_facts: Service root is the tenant url from the service key plus /api/v1; MessageProcessingLogs is a resource path under it.

The API implements OData specification version 2.0: collections arrive as d.results, counts as d.__count (string), server paging as a d.__next link carrying $skiptoken, and at most 1000 entries are returned per call.

Datetime filter literals use datetime'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss'; the API does not parse zone information and interprets all literals as UTC, so clients must fold offsets into UTC before building the literal.
The documented message Status values are COMPLETED, PROCESSING, RETRY, ESCALATED, FAILED, CANCELLED, DISCARDED, ABANDONED; CustomStatus is a separate flow-defined value.
Edm.DateTime property values serialize as /Date(milliseconds since epoch)/ in OData 2.0 JSON.
Un-expanded navigation properties appear as __deferred objects whose uri is followed as-is; the error text of a log is read from .../ErrorInformation/$value.

Reads are guarded by the MonitoringDataRead role template; the API is protected by basic authentication and OAuth; failed authentication is HTTP 401 and missing authorization surfaces an OData error document.
OData 2.0 error documents have error.code and error.message.{lang,value}; SAP tenants append vendor diagnostic members (for example innererror) that clients must preserve.
